dsVertClient 1.1.0
    • Overview
    • Generalised linear models
    • GLM (binomial, K=3)
    • NB (over-dispersed Poisson, K=3)
    • Survival
    • Cox PH (K=2 OT-Beaver)
    • Cox PH (K=3 Allison-Poisson trick)
    • Mixed models / longitudinal
    • LMM (K=2 closed-form REML)
    • LMM (K=3 REML profile + within-between)
    • GEE (K=3 sandwich)
    • GLMM (K=3 binomial Laplace)
    • Categorical
    • Ordinal joint Newton (K=2 Tutz)
    • Ordinal warm OVR (K=3)
    • Multinomial joint Newton (K=2 Bohning)
    • Multinomial warm OVR (K=3)
    • Causal / penalised
    • IPW (K=3 two-stage)
    • LASSO (K=3 proximal handoff)
  • Reference

One-step Newton Cox at beta=0 (client orchestrator)

Source: R/ds.vertCox.newton.R
dot-ds_vertCox_newton_one_step.Rd

Called by ds.vertCox with one_step_newton = TRUE. Pre-conditions (caller has already:) * standardised X via .glm_mpc_setup() * shared X (k2ShareInputDS / k2ReceiveShareDS) * registered Cox times (k2SetCoxTimesDS / k2ReceiveCoxMetaDS) * applied permutation (k2ApplyCoxPermutationDS) so ss$k2_x_share_fp, ss$k2_peer_x_share_fp, ss$k2_cox_delta_fp are all sorted by time on both parties.

Does: 1. dsvertCoxNewtonPrepDS on both parties (local extract + cumsum). 2. dsvertCoxNewtonGradDS on both parties -> aggregate grad vector. 3. For each Fisher pair (j <= k), run two Beaver vecmul protocols (one for the XX term, one for the SS term), then scalar aggregate. 4. Solve beta = solve(Fisher, grad). Returns std-scale beta.

Usage

.ds_vertCox_newton_one_step(
  datasources,
  server_names,
  server_list,
  y_server,
  nl,
  session_id,
  n_obs,
  transport_pks,
  p_coord,
  p_nl,
  .dsAgg = NULL,
  .sendBlob = NULL,
  verbose = TRUE,
  ring = 63L
)

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
